---
title: "Knowledge Base"
subtitle: "Reusable references for the whole course"
---

This is the course's **knowledge base**: standalone pages you can come back to during any week (or after the course). Weekly lessons link into this section; the material here is meant to outlive any single class.

## AI & LLM fundamentals

- [LLM Glossary](../da-knowledge/technical-terms-page.qmd) — technical terms used in lectures and class.
- [Which AI to use](../da-knowledge/which-ai.qmd) — January 2026 comparison of major models and tools.
- [Beyond the course](../da-knowledge/beyond.qmd) — curated videos, papers and further reading. Updated regularly.

## Setting up AI tools

- [Terminal Basics](../da-knowledge/terminal-basics.qmd) — essential shell commands for CLI work.
- [Installing AI CLI Tools](../da-knowledge/install-cli.qmd) — Claude Code, Gemini CLI, Codex CLI, OpenCode.
- [VS Code + Copilot setup](../da-knowledge/vscode-setup.qmd) — IDE + AI assistant.

## Working with APIs

- [How to get AI API keys](../da-knowledge/get-ai-api-key.qmd) — OpenAI and Anthropic.
- [Introduction to APIs](../da-knowledge/api-use.qmd) — fundamental concepts: what a call looks like, headers, auth, rate limits.
- [Calling LLM APIs from Python](../da-knowledge/llm-api-python.qmd) — practical first calls, helper functions, and `pandas` workflows.
- [How APIs work under the hood](../da-knowledge/api-advanced.qmd) — idempotency, retries, batching, tokens.
- [Walkthrough: World Bank + FRED](../da-knowledge/walkthrough-wb-fred.qmd) — gentle intro using public economic data APIs.
- [Walkthrough: FBref football data](../da-knowledge/walkthrough-fbref.qmd) — slightly harder end-to-end example.

## Doing the analysis

- [Designing larger analytics projects with AI](../da-knowledge/designing-projects.qmd) — mindsets, self-interview, `agents.md`, SKILLS, the three kinds of tests.
- [Documentation and READMEs](../da-knowledge/documentation-readme.qmd) — what good documentation looks like.
- [Joining data tables](../da-knowledge/joining-tables.qmd) — keys, joins, pitfalls.
- [Creating graphs](../da-knowledge/creating-graphs.qmd) — iterative refinement from basic to publication quality (R example).
- [PDF guide](../da-knowledge/pdf-guide.qmd) — working with PDFs.
- [Good report ideas](../week03/ideas-good-report.qmd) — what separates a solid report from a weak one.

## Case studies

Datasets and code organised by case study — used across multiple weeks.

1. [World Values Survey](../case-studies/VWS/) — Weeks 2–3 (documentation, reports).
2. [Austrian Hotels](../case-studies/austria-hotels/) — Week 4 (joining tables).
3. [Football Manager Interviews](../case-studies/interviews/) — Weeks 7–8 (text as data).
4. [US Earnings (CPS)](../case-studies/earnings/) — Week 6 (from data to report).
5. [Employee Commits](../case-studies/employee_commits/) — data-analysis demos.
6. [Common Support (R)](../case-studies/common-support-r/) — iterative AI coding.

[Browse all case studies →](../case-studies/)
